rejected_candidates.title: Large inactive holders can be frozen once reflections shrink `rTotal` below their stale `rOwned`
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: the implemented transfer and `reflect()` paths preserve the reflected-supply invariant, so an individual holder's `rOwned` does not naturally grow past global `rTotal` under the code shown.

rejected_candidates.title: `reflect()` can leave excess NFTs outstanding and temporarily untransferable
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: the stale NFT count is real after self-`reflect()`, but it is self-inflicted and can be reconciled by a later ERC20 transfer path; this does not clear the reportability bar for protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Reflection fee calculation bug causes underflow for excluded recipients
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: the excluded-recipient branch credits `rOwnedTo` with `rTransferAmount` and `tOwnedTo` with `tTransferAmount`, which is the correct post-fee accounting.

rejected_candidates.title: Backdoor allows Uniswap router to transfer from owner when trading is disabled
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: this is an explicit owner-only launch exception, and the owner is already separately allowed to transfer while trading is disabled; it is not an unauthorized bypass.

rejected_candidates.title: _transferFromNFT doesn't check tradingEnabled flag
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: the `DeezNutz` override does enforce a trading check. In fact, because NFT calls arrive from the mirror contract, pre-trading NFT transfers are blocked rather than bypassed.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can manipulate reflection rate through includeAccount
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: `includeAccount()` is already unusable because of the inverted `isExcluded` check, and `renounceFunctions()` never claims to renounce that function.

rejected_candidates.title: reflect function has no access control
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: `reflect()` is an intentional self-burn primitive that only affects the caller's own reflected balance and does not create a standalone exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Uniswap router address cannot be updated after deployment
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: router immutability is a deployment/configuration choice, not a security vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Incomplete renounceFunctions leaves admin functions accessible
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: the function comment explicitly says it renounces only `setTaxFee` and `excludeAccount`; retaining other owner powers is the documented design.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing return value in ERC20 approve function
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: `approve()` explicitly returns `true`, so the claimed ERC20 incompatibility is unsupported.

rejected_candidates.title: NFT transfer doesn't verify tradingEnabled status in mirror
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: the mirror delegates into the base contract, and the base override applies the trading gate there; there is no mirror-side bypass.

rejected_candidates.title: includeAccount lacks functionsRenounced check but excludeAccount has it
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: the asymmetry is not independently exploitable, and `includeAccount()` is already broken by the inverted exclusion check.
